title: Gigabrain: Memory OS for AI Agents
description: A guided tour through Gigabrain's architecture — from plugin entry point to capture pipelines, recall orchestration, world-model projection, and the Obsidian vault surface.
prerequisites: Familiarity with SQLite and event-sourcing patterns

steps.title: Welcome to Gigabrain
steps.description: Gigabrain is a long-term memory layer for OpenClaw agents. It captures memories from conversations, stores them in an event-sourced SQLite database, projects them into a structured world model, and surfaces them as an Obsidian vault. The codebase is organized into a plugin entry point, a core services library, CLI scripts, tests, and an optional web console.

steps.title: Plugin Entry Point
steps.description: index.ts is the OpenClaw plugin factory. It wires up event hooks and registers HTTP routes on the gateway. The plugin config file defines the full configuration schema — paths, capture settings, recall tuning, world-model options, and vault output.

interactiveActions.description: Click index.ts to see the plugin factory and event hooks

steps.title: Core Services Overview
steps.description: The lib core directory contains all service implementations. Key modules: config validates settings, capture service extracts memories, recall service searches and injects context, orchestrator selects recall strategies, world model builds entity, belief, and episode graphs, projection store manages SQLite state, and vault mirror generates the Obsidian surface.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over lib/core to see all service modules

steps.title: Capture Pipeline
steps.description: When an agent response completes, capture service extracts memory note tags and remember intent phrases. Candidates pass through the policy module for junk filtering and quality scoring, then get deduplicated and upserted into the projection store. Native promotion promotes chunks from memory files into structured registry entries.

interactiveActions.description: Click capture-service.js to see how memories are extracted from agent responses

steps.title: Recall & Orchestration
steps.description: Before each prompt, the orchestrator classifies the query into one of five strategies: quick context, entity brief, relationship brief, timeline brief, and verification lookup. The recall service executes the strategy with coreference resolution, temporal windows, and token budgeting. The LLM router abstracts over multiple providers.

interactiveActions.description: Click orchestrator.js to see the five recall strategies

steps.title: World Model & Persistence
steps.description: world-model.js projects memories into structured graphs: entities, beliefs, episodes, open loops, and syntheses. projection-store.js manages the SQLite schema with a materialized current-state view, while event-store.js maintains an append-only audit log. native-sync.js indexes MEMORY.md for hybrid recall.

interactiveActions.description: Click world-model.js to see entity/belief/episode projection

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description: vault-mirror.js generates a structured Obsidian vault surface. maintenance-service.js runs the nightly pipeline (sync, dedupe, audit, vacuum, vault build). scripts/ has the CLI control plane, tests/ covers unit through performance, and the memory api is an optional FastAPI web console.

interactiveActions.description: Explore the full codebase — click into any area that interests you
